content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Samuel Fineman, President 
Alhambra Metal Products 
322 South Date Avenue 
P.O. Box 829 
Alhambra, CA 
91802-0829 
Dear Mr. Fineman: 
January 13, 1988 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal 
Assistance 
Our File No. I-88-025 
We have received your inquiry concerning provisions of law 
which require legislators and state employees to devote their 
time and energy to the jobs that they were elected or hired to 
perform. 
As we advised you in our previous letter, the 
Political Reform Act (Government Code Sections 81000-91015) 
does not contain any such provision. 
The jurisdiction of the 
Fair Political Practices commission is limited to the 
provisions of the Political Reform Act. 
Because your question 
does not concern a matter addressed by the Political Reform 
Act, we cannot assist you further. 
Complaints about misuse of 
public funds should be addressed to the State Attorney 
General's office. 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660 


Januru:y 5, 1988 
CALIFORNIA FAIR POLITICAL PRACl'ICES CCM1ISSION 
428 "J" Street, P.O. Box 807 
Sacran:ento, CA 
95804-0807 
A'ITN: 
Ms. Kathryn E. Donovan, Counsel/Legal Division 
REF: 
Our Request for Infonnal Assistance/Your File # 1-87-310 
Dear Ms. Donovan: 
Thank you for your letter of December II, 1987, which I received December 
16, 1987. 
You state that, "There is no provision in the Political Reform Act 
that vmuld require Assemblyman Bro;v:n to take an unpaid leave of absence from 
the Legislature while managing the presidential campaiqn. 
We are unable to 
advise you whether there are other laws which might apply to this situation". 
This may be true, hCMever, since there is no provision in that particular 
referenced Act, is there any other law that would require our law-makers and 
State errployees to devote their time and energy to the jobs that they were 
elected or hired to perfonn for us? Also, because of t.~e subject matter, ie. 
Assemblyman Willie Bro;v:n's becoming the Campaign Manager for Jesse Jackson's 
Presidential Campaign, has Mr. Bro;v:n made any public, formal, informal or 
private cammittment that he will not stay on the State payroll and use State 
expense account allCMances, while devoting all of his attention to the Jackson 
campaign? 
This is an issue that I think should be closely rronitored in our State. 
We should not allCM State enployees of any kind to be on our payroll and not 
perform their jobs. 
I await your answer. 
SF/gt 
Samuel Fineman 
President 
cc: 
Governor George Deukmejian 
State Attorney GeDeral 
Republican Central Carmi ttee 
Derrocratic Central Carmittee 
Los Angeles Chamber of Corrrnerce 
Alharrbra Charrber 
Ccmrerce

sections.conclusion: The Political Reform Act (Government Code Sections 81000-91015) does not contain any provision requiring legislators and state employees to devote their time and energy to their jobs. The FPPC's jurisdiction is limited to the Political Reform Act, and because the question does not concern a matter addressed by that Act, the FPPC cannot provide further assistance. Complaints about misuse of public funds should be addressed to the State Attorney General's office.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Political Reform Act requires legislators and state employees to devote their time and energy exclusively to their elected or hired positions, and whether Assemblyman Willie Brown must take unpaid leave while serving as campaign manager for Jesse Jackson's presidential campaign.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that the Political Reform Act contains no provision requiring legislators and state employees to devote their time exclusively to their positions, and this matter falls outside the FPPC's jurisdiction under the Act. The inquirer is directed to the State Attorney General's office for questions about misuse of public funds.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request letter from Samuel Fineman (dated January 5, 1988) and the FPPC response letter from Diane M. Griffiths (dated January 13, 1988). The incoming request references a previous FPPC letter and follows up with additional questions. File number I-88-025 appears in the response letter header.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the Political Reform Act requires legislators and state employees to devote their time and energy exclusively to their elected or hired positions, and whether Assemblyman Willie Brown must take unpaid leave while serving as campaign manager for Jesse Jackson's presidential campaign.

CONCLUSION: The Political Reform Act (Government Code Sections 81000-91015) does not contain any provision requiring legislators and state employees to devote their time and energy to their jobs. The FPPC's jurisdiction is limited to the Political Reform Act, and because the question does not concern a matter addressed by that Act, the FPPC cannot provide further assistance. Complaints about misuse of public funds should be addressed to the State Attorney General's office.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to Samuel Fineman's inquiry about whether legislators and state employees are required to devote full time to their positions under the Political Reform Act, declining jurisdiction as the Act does not address this matter.
